title: Why Your Office Needs an Air Purifier
description: Air purifier guide for Malaysian offices and home offices. Slim models for desks, tower units for meeting rooms, and floor-standing for pantries. From RM50/mo.
excerpt: Office workers in Malaysia spend 8 or more hours in air-conditioned spaces with recycled air. Here is how to choose the right air purifier for a home office, a meeting room, or a shared commercial space.
body:
If you work in an office in Malaysia — whether a home office, a co-working space, or a commercial building — you are breathing recirculated air-conditioned air for 8 or more hours every day. Most office aircond systems use basic mesh filters that catch dust and lint but let fine particles, VOCs and allergens pass through freely. Adding a dedicated air purifier to your workspace improves the air you breathe during the longest continuous exposure window of your day.

This guide covers why office air quality matters, how to choose the right purifier for different office spaces, and which LG PuriCare models fit each use case.

## Why office air quality is often worse than you think

Office environments have specific air quality challenges that differ from residential spaces.

**High occupancy density.** More people in a confined space means more CO2, more bioaerosols (respiratory droplets, skin cells), and more airborne bacteria. A meeting room with 8 people and the door closed for an hour can see CO2 levels rise above 1,000 ppm — the threshold where concentration and cognitive performance measurably decline.

**Centralised HVAC without HEPA.** Most Malaysian office buildings use centralised air handling units with G4 or F7 pre-filters. These are designed to protect the HVAC equipment, not to purify the air for occupants. Fine particles, mould spores and allergens recirculate through the ductwork.

**Off-gassing from furniture and materials.** Office carpets, particleboard desks, synthetic chairs and printers release formaldehyde and other VOCs continuously. New office fit-outs are particularly heavy with off-gassing in the first 6-12 months.

**Pantry and food odours.** In Malaysian offices, the pantry is rarely separated by a sealed door. Microwaved curry, nasi lemak steam and coffee machine fumes travel through the open-plan space.

## Air purifier recommendations by office type

### Home office (10-20 m2)

A home office is typically a spare bedroom or a partitioned area in the living room. You are the only occupant, and the main concerns are recirculated aircond air, dust, and allergens.

The [LG PuriCare AeroBooster](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster) is purpose-built for this space. At 240 mm wide, it fits beside a desk without taking up floor space. The dual H13 HEPA filters and BAF allergy certification handle dust, pollen and haze particles. Clean Booster pushes purified air up to 8.5 metres across the room.

[![LG PuriCare AeroBooster — 240mm slim, fits beside any desk](/uploads/products/aero-booster/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster)

At RM65/month on LG Subscribe, the AeroBooster covers 52.8 m2 — more than enough for any home office. Run it on auto mode during working hours and it will maintain clean air levels without you thinking about it.

Alternatively, the [AeroFurniture at RM60/month](/products/air-purifiers/aero-furniture) doubles as a side table with Qi wireless charging. If your home office setup needs a surface for your phone or a cup of coffee beside the desk, this model saves furniture and serves dual duty.

[![LG PuriCare AeroFurniture — side table with wireless charging for home offices](/uploads/products/aero-furniture/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-furniture)

### Meeting room (15-30 m2)

Meeting rooms are the worst indoor air quality hotspots in any office. Multiple people, closed doors, limited ventilation, and sessions that run an hour or more. CO2 and bioaerosol levels climb rapidly.

The [LG 360 HIT at RM50/month](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) covers 61.2 m2 with 360-degree air intake — it can be placed in a corner or against a wall and still draw air from every direction. At 20 dB on low speed, it will not interfere with discussions. The Clean Booster circulates purified air across the full room.

[![LG PuriCare 360 HIT — 360-degree intake for meeting rooms](/uploads/products/puricare-360-hit/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit)

For larger meeting rooms or boardrooms (30-60 m2), the [Alpha Pet Single at RM75/month](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-single) provides 61.2 m2 coverage with stronger CADR. The "Pet" designation refers to the enhanced filter — the unit works identically for office use.

### Open-plan office (50-100 m2)

Large open-plan offices need higher CADR and wider coverage. A single purifier in the corner will not cycle the air effectively across a 100 m2 space.

Two practical approaches:

**Option 1: One large unit.** The [Alpha Pet Double at RM105/month](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double) covers 104 m2 with dual H13 HEPA filters and the highest CADR in the LG range. Place it centrally in the open-plan area.

**Option 2: Multiple smaller units.** Place two or three [360 HIT units at RM50/month each](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) in different zones of the open-plan area. This provides better air distribution and covers more dead zones than a single central unit. Total cost: RM100-150/month for comprehensive coverage.

Multiple smaller units is often the better approach for rectangular open-plan offices where a single unit cannot reach both ends effectively.

### Office pantry

The pantry is a persistent odour source in Malaysian offices. Microwaved food, coffee machines and the bin produce a steady stream of cooking fumes and organic gases.

A compact purifier with strong deodorising capability works here. The [360 HIT at RM50/month](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) has a three-stage deodorising filter designed for cooking and food odours — the same recommendation we make for [open-plan kitchens in homes](/blog/air-purifier-open-plan-kitchen-cooking-odour/).

Place it at least 2 metres from the microwave and sink to avoid steam and splatter damage. Run it on auto mode throughout office hours.

For an office of 10 employees, even a 1% improvement in productivity from cleaner air is worth more than RM600/year. The purifier also reduces sick days — airborne virus transmission drops when HEPA filtration removes respiratory droplets from the shared air.

During haze season, the business case becomes even clearer. Employees with asthma or allergies who might otherwise take sick leave can continue working comfortably in a HEPA-filtered office.

title: Aircond HP Guide: What Size for Your Room
description: How to choose the right aircond HP for your room in Malaysia. Room size to HP calculation table, plus all 6 LG models with pricing. Plans from RM80/month.
excerpt: Getting the HP wrong is the most common aircond mistake in Malaysia. This guide shows you exactly how to match room size to horsepower — with a simple table and all 6 LG DUAL Inverter models compared.
body:
Choosing the right aircond horsepower for your room is the single most important decision in the buying process. An undersized unit runs at full load all day, never reaches the target temperature, and drives your TNB bill up. An oversized unit short-cycles — switching on and off repeatedly — which wastes energy, creates uneven cooling, and shortens the compressor's lifespan. This guide gives you a clear room size to HP formula for Malaysian conditions, plus the full LG DUAL Inverter range with pricing.

## The basic formula

The general calculation for Malaysian homes uses BTU (British Thermal Unit) requirements based on room size, then converts to HP.

**Step 1: Calculate room area in square feet.** Length (ft) x Width (ft) = Room area (sq ft).

**Step 2: Multiply by 65 BTU per sq ft.** This is the baseline for Malaysian climate — year-round tropical heat, standard 9-10 ft ceiling height, and moderate sun exposure.

**Step 3: Adjust for conditions.** Add 10-20% if the room gets direct afternoon sun, has large glass windows, is on the top floor, or has a ceiling higher than 10 ft. Subtract 10% if the room is shaded, has minimal windows, or is on a lower floor of a high-rise.

**Step 4: Match BTU to HP.** Use the conversion table below.

| HP | BTU/hr | Room size (sq ft) | Room size (m2) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1.0 HP | 9,000 | 100-150 | 9-14 |
| 1.5 HP | 12,000 | 150-250 | 14-23 |
| 2.0 HP | 18,000 | 250-400 | 23-37 |
| 2.5 HP | 24,000 | 400-550 | 37-51 |

## Quick room size to HP table

If you do not want to calculate BTU, use this simplified guide.

| Room type | Typical size (sq ft) | Typical size (m2) | Recommended HP |
|---|---|---|---|
| Small bedroom / study | 80-150 | 7-14 | 1.0 HP |
| Standard bedroom | 120-200 | 11-19 | 1.0-1.5 HP |
| Master bedroom | 150-270 | 14-25 | 1.5 HP |
| Master bedroom (large) | 250-350 | 23-33 | 1.5-2.0 HP |
| Small living room | 200-300 | 19-28 | 1.5-2.0 HP |
| Medium living room | 300-400 | 28-37 | 2.0 HP |
| Large living room | 400-550 | 37-51 | 2.5 HP |
| Open-plan living + dining | 500-700 | 46-65 | 2.5 HP (or 2 units) |

These are guidelines for standard Malaysian conditions. Adjust up if your room has specific heat factors.

## Factors that increase cooling load in Malaysia

Seven conditions common in Malaysian homes that require you to size up by 0.5 HP.

**Direct afternoon sun.** West-facing rooms absorb significantly more heat between 2pm and 6pm. If the room has large west-facing windows, add 0.5 HP.

**Top floor / no insulation above.** The top floor of a landed house or the highest floor unit in a low-rise condo absorbs roof heat. This can add 15-25% to the cooling requirement.

**Large glass windows or sliding doors.** Floor-to-ceiling windows are common in Malaysian condos. Glass transmits heat faster than walls, so a room with extensive glazing needs more cooling capacity.

**High ceilings.** A room with a 12 ft ceiling has 20% more air volume than a room with a 10 ft ceiling. The aircond needs more capacity to cool the extra volume.

**Kitchen heat.** If the aircond cools an open-plan space that includes the kitchen, residual heat from cooking appliances adds to the load. Consider sizing up or using a separate unit for the kitchen area.

**Many occupants.** Each person generates approximately 400 BTU/hr of body heat. A living room that regularly hosts 6-8 people needs more capacity than the same room used by 2 people.

**Electronic equipment.** A home office with multiple monitors, a desktop PC and a laser printer generates measurable heat. Add 10% if the room has significant electronics.

## The 6 LG DUAL Inverter aircond models

All LG airconds in this range use DUAL Inverter compressor technology with a 10-year warranty, Plasmaster ionisation, and R32 refrigerant.

| Model | HP | Best for | From |
|---|---|---|---|
| [1.0HP DUAL Inverter](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-1hp-s3q09) | 1.0 | Small bedrooms, study rooms | RM80/mo |
| [1.5HP DUAL Inverter](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-1-5hp-s3q12) | 1.5 | Master bedrooms, small living rooms | RM90/mo |
| [ARTCOOL 1.5HP](/products/air-conditioners/artcool-1-5hp-s3q12r) | 1.5 | Design-conscious bedrooms | RM100/mo |
| [2.0HP DUAL Inverter](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-2hp-s3q18) | 2.0 | Medium living rooms | RM110/mo |
| [2.5HP DUAL Inverter](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-2-5hp-s3q24) | 2.5 | Large living rooms | RM120/mo |
| [ARTCOOL 2.5HP](/products/air-conditioners/artcool-2-5hp-s3q24r) | 2.5 | Large living rooms, design-conscious | RM130/mo |

[![LG 2.0HP DUAL Inverter — covers medium living rooms up to 37 m2](/uploads/products/dual-inverter-2hp-s3q18/main.avif)](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-2hp-s3q18)

## Standard vs ARTCOOL: which to choose

The standard DUAL Inverter and ARTCOOL models share identical cooling performance at the same HP level. The difference is design: ARTCOOL replaces the white plastic indoor unit with a mirror-glass panel that blends into modern interiors.

The price premium is RM10/month — RM100 vs RM90 for 1.5HP, and RM130 vs RM120 for 2.5HP. If the indoor unit is mounted in a visible position (above the sofa, facing the bed), the ARTCOOL is worth the premium. For utility rooms, spare bedrooms and spaces where aesthetics are secondary, the standard model saves RM10/month.

[![LG ARTCOOL 1.5HP — mirror glass panel for design-conscious interiors](/uploads/products/artcool-1-5hp-s3q12r/main.avif)](/products/air-conditioners/artcool-1-5hp-s3q12r)

## Sample room calculations

**Example 1: Master bedroom in a condo.** Room measures 14 ft x 16 ft = 224 sq ft. Standard ceiling, no direct sun. 224 x 65 = 14,560 BTU. This falls in the 1.5 HP range. Recommendation: [1.5HP DUAL Inverter at RM90/month](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-1-5hp-s3q12).

**Example 2: Living room in a double-storey terrace.** Room measures 20 ft x 18 ft = 360 sq ft. West-facing windows with afternoon sun. 360 x 65 = 23,400 BTU + 20% sun adjustment = 28,080 BTU. This exceeds 2.0 HP and needs 2.5 HP. Recommendation: [2.5HP DUAL Inverter at RM120/month](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-2-5hp-s3q24).

**Example 3: Child's bedroom.** Room measures 10 ft x 12 ft = 120 sq ft. No direct sun, lower floor. 120 x 65 = 7,800 BTU. This is comfortably within 1.0 HP range. Recommendation: [1.0HP DUAL Inverter at RM80/month](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-1hp-s3q09).

**Example 4: Open-plan living and dining.** Combined area measures 22 ft x 28 ft = 616 sq ft. High ceiling (12 ft), some west-facing glass. 616 x 65 = 40,040 BTU + 25% adjustment = 50,050 BTU. This exceeds any single wall-mounted split unit. Consider two units: a [2.5HP for the living zone at RM120/month](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-2-5hp-s3q24) and a [1.5HP for the dining zone at RM90/month](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-1-5hp-s3q12).

## Common HP mistakes to avoid

**Buying 1.0HP for a master bedroom.** Many Malaysian master bedrooms are 180-250 sq ft — too large for 1.0HP to cool effectively. The unit runs at full load all day, consumes more electricity than a correctly sized 1.5HP at variable speed, and never reaches target temperature. Size up to 1.5HP.

**Buying 2.5HP for a small bedroom.** An oversized unit cools the room in minutes, then shuts off. When the temperature rises slightly, it blasts on again. This short-cycling creates cold-hot swings, increases wear on the compressor, and uses more electricity than a correctly sized unit maintaining steady output.

**Ignoring sun exposure.** A shaded 300 sq ft room and a sun-blasted 300 sq ft room have very different cooling loads. Always account for window orientation and direct sunlight.

## What is included on LG Subscribe

Every aircond on LG Subscribe includes free delivery, professional installation of both indoor and outdoor units, a 10-year compressor warranty, and annual servicing (chemical wash and performance check). You do not pay upfront and there is no deposit.

Plans run 60 months (5 years). At the end of the term, the unit is yours. You can also choose to upgrade to a newer model at that point.

- [TNB Tariff 2026: What It Means for You](/blog/tnb-tariff-2026-what-new-rates-mean-for-home/)

Not sure which HP your room needs? Send us your room dimensions and window orientation on WhatsApp. Our consultants will calculate the BTU requirement and recommend the right LG model — no guesswork, no overselling.

title: Subscribe to an Aircond in Malaysia
description: Should you subscribe, buy or hire-purchase an aircond in Malaysia? We compare all three options on cost, warranty, maintenance and flexibility. From RM80/mo.
excerpt: Subscription, outright purchase and hire-purchase are three ways to get an aircond in Malaysia. We compare the real costs, warranty terms, maintenance and flexibility of each so you can choose the right path.
body:
Getting a new aircond in Malaysia used to mean one decision: which brand and HP. Now there is a second decision — how you pay for it. You can buy outright, finance through hire-purchase, or subscribe through a service like LG Subscribe. Each option has different implications for your cash flow, warranty coverage, maintenance and flexibility. This post compares all three so you can choose the option that fits your financial situation.

## The three options at a glance

| Factor | Buy outright | Hire-purchase | LG Subscribe |
|---|---|---|---|
| Upfront cost | Full price (RM2,000-5,000+) | Deposit (10-20%) | RM0 |
| Monthly payment | None | Fixed instalment | Fixed subscription |
| Interest | None | 3-8% p.a. | None (flat rate) |
| Warranty | 1-2 years (standard) | 1-2 years (standard) | Full term (60 months) |
| Installation | Separate cost (RM200-600) | Sometimes included | Included |
| Maintenance | On you | On you | Included (annual service) |
| Ownership | Immediate | After full payment | After term ends |
| Flexibility to upgrade | Sell old unit yourself | Pay off remaining, then sell | Upgrade at end of term |

## Option 1: Buy outright

Buying outright means paying the full retail price in one transaction. For a 1.5HP inverter aircond, this is typically RM2,000-3,500 depending on the brand. Installation is quoted separately — expect RM250-500 for standard installation (indoor unit, outdoor unit, piping up to 10 ft).

The advantage is clear: no monthly commitment, no interest, and the unit is yours immediately. If you have cash available and plan to keep the aircond for 8-10 years, this is the cheapest option in total ringgit spent.

The disadvantages: your cash is tied up in a depreciating asset, the manufacturer warranty is typically 1-2 years for the unit (compressor warranty may be longer), and all maintenance and repairs after the warranty period are out of pocket. A typical chemical wash costs RM80-150 per unit per year. A compressor replacement (rare but expensive) runs RM800-1,500.

## Option 2: Hire-purchase or credit card instalment

Hire-purchase spreads the cost over 12-36 months through a financing arrangement — either directly through the retailer, a bank, or 0% credit card instalments.

The monthly payment depends on the total price and the financing term. A RM3,000 aircond on 24-month 0% instalment is RM125/month. With interest-bearing hire-purchase at 5% p.a., the same unit costs approximately RM131/month over 24 months (total: RM3,150).

The advantage over outright purchase: you keep your cash. The disadvantage compared to subscription: you still pay for installation separately, the warranty is still 1-2 years (standard), and maintenance is your responsibility after the warranty expires. Credit card instalments also tie up your credit limit for the full instalment period.

## Option 3: LG Subscribe

LG Subscribe is a monthly subscription that bundles the aircond, delivery, installation, warranty and annual maintenance into one fixed payment for 60 months.

| Model | Monthly | Term | Included |
|---|---|---|---|
| [1.0HP DUAL Inverter](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-1hp-s3q09) | RM80/mo | 60 mo | Unit + install + 10-year compressor warranty + annual service |
| [1.5HP DUAL Inverter](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-1-5hp-s3q12) | RM90/mo | 60 mo | Same |
| [ARTCOOL 1.5HP](/products/air-conditioners/artcool-1-5hp-s3q12r) | RM100/mo | 60 mo | Same |
| [2.0HP DUAL Inverter](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-2hp-s3q18) | RM110/mo | 60 mo | Same |
| [2.5HP DUAL Inverter](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-2-5hp-s3q24) | RM120/mo | 60 mo | Same |
| [ARTCOOL 2.5HP](/products/air-conditioners/artcool-2-5hp-s3q24r) | RM130/mo | 60 mo | Same |

[![LG 1.5HP DUAL Inverter — RM90/month with installation and annual service included](/uploads/products/dual-inverter-1-5hp-s3q12/main.avif)](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-1-5hp-s3q12)

There is no deposit, no upfront cost, and no interest. The monthly rate is fixed for the full 60 months. Installation of both indoor and outdoor units is included. Annual servicing (chemical wash, gas check, performance inspection) is included. The 10-year compressor warranty covers the most expensive component of the aircond for the full expected lifespan.

At the end of the 60-month term, the unit is yours. You can continue using it, upgrade to a newer model, or return it.

## Real cost comparison: 1.5HP aircond over 5 years

Let us compare the three options for a 1.5HP inverter aircond over 5 years — the typical minimum lifespan before most families consider replacing.

| Cost item | Buy outright | Hire-purchase (24 mo, 0%) | LG Subscribe (60 mo) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Unit price | RM3,000 | RM3,000 | Included |
| Installation | RM350 | RM350 | Included |
| Annual service x 5 | RM500-750 | RM500-750 | Included |
| Repairs (years 3-5) | RM0-500 (variable) | RM0-500 (variable) | Included |
| Interest | RM0 | RM0 (0% promo) | RM0 |
| **5-year total** | **RM3,850-4,600** | **RM3,850-4,600** | **RM5,400** |

On raw ringgit, buying outright or hire-purchase is cheaper by RM800-1,550 over 5 years. But the subscription total includes every service visit, every repair, and a 10-year compressor warranty — costs that are variable and unpredictable under the other two options.

The real question is not which costs less in total, but which fits your cash flow and risk preference.

## When subscribe makes more sense than buying

**You are furnishing a new home.** If you need aircond for 3 bedrooms and a living room, that is RM12,000-15,000 outright plus RM1,000-2,000 for installation. On LG Subscribe, the same 4-unit setup is RM350-400/month with zero upfront. Your renovation budget stays intact.

**You want zero maintenance admin.** LG proactively schedules annual servicing. You do not need to find a contractor, negotiate pricing, or remember when the last chemical wash was done. For busy professionals and families, this convenience has real value.

**You want full-term warranty protection.** A compressor failure in year 3 or 4 on an outright-purchased unit can cost RM800-1,500 out of pocket. On LG Subscribe, it is covered. The 10-year compressor warranty eliminates the most expensive possible repair.

**You plan to upgrade in 5 years.** If you want the latest energy-efficient model every 5 years rather than running the same unit for a decade, subscription gives you a clean upgrade path. No selling old units, no negotiating trade-in value.

## When buying outright makes more sense

**You have cash and no competing priorities.** If RM3,000-5,000 does not strain your finances and you plan to keep the aircond for 8-10 years, outright purchase is cheaper in total.

**You only need one unit.** A single aircond is a manageable one-time expense. Subscription is most valuable when you need multiple units simultaneously.

**You are comfortable managing your own maintenance.** If you already have a trusted aircond contractor and you service on schedule, the maintenance inclusion in the subscription plan has less value for you.

## The flexibility factor

One advantage of subscription that is easy to overlook: flexibility. If you move house during the term, LG Subscribe can arrange relocation of the unit. If your circumstances change and you need to end the plan early, early settlement is an option. If the unit develops a fault, LG repairs or replaces it — you do not need to diagnose, source parts, or negotiate with a contractor.

Buying outright gives you ownership but ties you to the unit. Hire-purchase gives you financing but ties you to the debt. Subscription gives you the appliance, the maintenance and the warranty — with a clear end date and a clean upgrade path.

title: Aircond Running Cost: TNB Bill Breakdown
description: How much does an aircond cost to run per month in Malaysia? We calculate TNB electricity costs for 1HP to 2.5HP inverter models at 2026 tariff rates.
excerpt: Your aircond is the biggest item on your TNB bill. We calculate the real monthly electricity cost for 1HP to 2.5HP inverter models under 2026 tariff rates — plus five tips to reduce your bill without sweating.
body:
The air conditioner is the single largest electricity consumer in most Malaysian homes. Depending on the HP, usage hours and whether the unit is inverter or non-inverter, your aircond can account for 40 to 60% of your monthly TNB bill. This post breaks down the real running cost per HP level under TNB's 2026 residential tariff so you can budget accurately and make informed decisions about usage.

## TNB 2026 residential tariff recap

TNB's 2026 residential tariff uses a tiered structure. For most Malaysian households running aircond, the effective rate that matters is the upper tier: 44.43 sen per kWh for consumption above 600 kWh/month.

Most families with aircond in 2 or more rooms exceed 600 kWh monthly. For this analysis, we use the 44.43 sen/kWh rate as the baseline — it represents the marginal cost of every additional kWh your aircond consumes.

## How aircond electricity consumption works

An aircond's rated wattage tells you the maximum power draw — but in practice, the unit rarely runs at maximum. An inverter aircond ramps the compressor to full power to reach the target temperature, then reduces to 30-50% capacity to maintain it. A non-inverter unit runs at 100% until the room is cool, shuts off completely, then restarts at 100% when the temperature rises.

The average power draw over an 8-hour session is what determines your TNB bill. For inverter units, this is typically 40-60% of rated wattage. For non-inverter units, it is 70-85% because the frequent restarts consume more energy than steady-state running.

## Monthly electricity cost by HP: inverter vs non-inverter

Based on 8 hours of daily use at TNB's 44.43 sen/kWh rate.

| HP | Rated wattage | Avg draw (inverter) | Monthly cost (inverter) | Avg draw (non-inverter) | Monthly cost (non-inverter) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1.0 HP | ~750W | ~375W | RM40-55 | ~600W | RM65-85 |
| 1.5 HP | ~1,100W | ~550W | RM60-80 | ~880W | RM95-130 |
| 2.0 HP | ~1,500W | ~750W | RM80-110 | ~1,200W | RM130-175 |
| 2.5 HP | ~2,000W | ~1,000W | RM105-145 | ~1,600W | RM175-235 |

The ranges account for variables like room insulation, outdoor temperature, thermostat setting and how well the room is sealed. The lower end assumes good insulation, 24-25 degrees Celsius setting, and minimal heat gain. The upper end assumes west-facing windows, lower thermostat settings, or poor insulation.

## The inverter advantage in ringgit

The table above makes the case clearly. An inverter 1.5HP unit costs RM60-80/month to run. A non-inverter 1.5HP unit costs RM95-130/month. That is RM35-50/month in savings — RM420-600 per year — from one aircond.

If you have 3 airconds in your home (master bedroom, living room, second bedroom), the annual savings from switching all three to inverter could be RM1,200-1,800. Over 5 years, that is RM6,000-9,000 — more than enough to pay for the aircond units themselves.

Every LG aircond in the Subscribe range uses DUAL Inverter technology. The [1.5HP DUAL Inverter at RM90/month](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-1-5hp-s3q12) and the [2.5HP DUAL Inverter at RM120/month](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-2-5hp-s3q24) are the two most popular models for Malaysian homes.

[![LG 2.5HP DUAL Inverter — the most popular choice for Malaysian living rooms](/uploads/products/dual-inverter-2-5hp-s3q24/main.avif)](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-2-5hp-s3q24)

## Monthly cost at different usage levels

Not everyone runs aircond for 8 hours. Here is the cost range for inverter models at different daily usage levels.

| HP | 4 hrs/day | 8 hrs/day | 12 hrs/day | 16 hrs/day |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1.0 HP | RM20-28 | RM40-55 | RM60-82 | RM80-110 |
| 1.5 HP | RM30-40 | RM60-80 | RM90-120 | RM120-160 |
| 2.0 HP | RM40-55 | RM80-110 | RM120-165 | RM160-220 |
| 2.5 HP | RM53-73 | RM105-145 | RM158-218 | RM210-290 |

Bedroom usage is typically 6-8 hours (overnight). Living room usage varies — some families run aircond all day (12-16 hours), while others use it only during the afternoon and evening (4-6 hours).

## Your total aircond TNB cost: a typical household

Let us estimate the total monthly aircond electricity cost for a typical 3-bedroom Malaysian home.

| Room | Model | Daily use | Est. monthly cost |
|---|---|---|---|
| Master bedroom | [1.5HP Inverter](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-1-5hp-s3q12) | 8 hrs | ~RM70 |
| Bedroom 2 | [1.0HP Inverter](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-1hp-s3q09) | 6 hrs | ~RM30 |
| Living room | [2.5HP Inverter](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-2-5hp-s3q24) | 6 hrs | ~RM80 |
| **Total aircond electricity** | | | **~RM180/month** |

Add RM50-80 for your fridge, washer, lights and other appliances, and a typical total TNB bill for this household is RM230-260/month. The aircond alone accounts for roughly 70% of the bill.

## Five tips to reduce aircond running cost

**1. Set the thermostat to 24-25 degrees Celsius.** Every 1 degree lower than 24 degrees Celsius increases energy consumption by approximately 6-10%. Running at 22 degrees instead of 24 degrees can add RM15-30/month per unit. Most people cannot feel the difference between 23 and 24 degrees after 15 minutes.

**2. Use the timer function.** If you sleep 7 hours, set the aircond to turn off after 5 hours. The room retains cool air for 1-2 hours after the unit stops. This saves 2 hours of running time every night — approximately RM15-25/month for a 1.5HP unit.

**3. Keep the aircond filter clean.** A dirty filter restricts airflow and forces the compressor to work 15-25% harder. Wash the mesh filter every 2 weeks. On [LG Subscribe plans](/products/air-conditioners), annual professional servicing (chemical wash, gas top-up, performance check) is included — this keeps the unit at peak efficiency year-round.

**4. Seal the room.** Close doors and windows when the aircond is running. An unsealed room loses cold air and forces the unit to work continuously at higher output. Even a gap under the door allows warm air infiltration.

**5. Use a fan to circulate cool air.** A ceiling fan or standing fan distributes cold air more evenly, allowing you to raise the thermostat by 1-2 degrees without losing comfort. Running a fan costs RM8-15/month — less than the savings from raising the thermostat.

## Subscription cost vs electricity cost

Here is the complete monthly picture for each LG aircond on Subscribe — subscription plus estimated electricity.

| Model | Subscribe | Est. electricity (8 hrs/day) | Total monthly |
|---|---|---|---|
| [1.0HP DUAL Inverter](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-1hp-s3q09) | RM80 | ~RM45 | ~RM125 |

## The bottom line on aircond electricity cost

Your aircond running cost depends on three things you control: the HP you choose (match it to your room size — see our [HP sizing guide](/blog/aircond-hp-size-guide-malaysia/)), the thermostat setting (24-25 degrees is the sweet spot), and how well you maintain the unit (clean filters, annual servicing).

Switching from non-inverter to inverter is the single largest saving available. For a household with 3 airconds, the switch saves RM1,200-1,800 per year in TNB costs. On LG Subscribe, the [1.0HP starts at RM80/month](/products/air-conditioners/dual-inverter-1hp-s3q09) with the 10-year compressor warranty and annual servicing included.
